title: init_swtpm returns libc -1 disguised as wolfTPM error code
description: When libc::setenv fails in swtpm.rs (e.g. ENOMEM), init_swtpm returns Err(-1). Callers treat this as a wolfTPM return code: wolftpm maps it to TpmRc(0xFFFFFFFF), wolftpm-tss maps it to Error::Transport { code: -1 }. Neither caller can distinguish this from a real wolfTPM error. The function's doc comment claims Err(rc) is always 'the nonzero wolfTPM return code from wolfTPM2_Init', which is false for this path. Fix: define a dedicated error type for init_swtpm, or return a distinct sentinel.
